mixer.name: mixer
mixer.description: A piece of farm equipment that mixes all the loaded ingredients for a feed recipe. Mobile mixers are most common, but stationary mixers also exist.

RAC.name: RAC
RAC.description: Release Access Code. This code is provided by AgSource and allows PULSE Platform to access your herd database.

STF.name: STF
STF.description: Standard Transfer File. This is a text file provided by a data records processing center (DRPC) that contains a list of your animals, including their IDs, reproduction status, and milk testing records. The STF is most commonly used to transfer records from one animal database to another. STF can also be used in PULSE to import animal data.

protocol.name: protocol
protocol.description: A dairy protocols include a series of treatments or tasks that follow a prescribed order and schedule. Protocols may be defined for reproduction purposes, to treat an illness, or for routine vaccinations.

zone.name: zone
zone.description: The term "Zone" represents different dairy locations. Platform Zones may be different physical dairy locations (for example, multiple locations across the state or country) or different user-defined locations within a single dairy site (for example, Pens 1-100 are Zone 1, Pens 101-200 are Zone 2, etc.).

worklist.name: worklist
worklist.description: A worklist is a structured list of tasks or animals requiring attention, typically used to organize and prioritize work. In PULSE and the mobile app, worklists identify animals that need to be seen by dairy workers or animal health experts for specific reasons, such as pregnancy checks or treatment in a hospital pen.

ISO.name: ISO
ISO.description: International Organization for Standardization, a global organization that promotes commercial and industrial standards.

EID.name: EID
EID.description: 15-digit electronic ID number associated with the animal eartag

care package.name: care package
care package.description: VAS uses the term "Care Package" to describe the updates that the Platform "packages up" and sends to the mobile app. The Care Package is used to keep the mobile app updated with the most recent on-premise data received by the Platform.

Task List.name: Task List
Task List.description: A task list in PULSE and the mobile app is a special type of worklist that is automatically generated for MyDC dairies to capture today's tasks for your herd. The Open Tasks list shows tasks from multiple sources, like your Dairy Protocols and integrations with heat and health partners, and helps you complete all required animal activities for the current day. Task lists include all OvSynch protocols, wellness checks, and any other scheduled tasks.

mode.name: mode
mode.description: The value that appears most often in a set of data.

Worklist.name: Worklist
Worklist.description: A worklist is a structured list of tasks or animals requiring attention, typically used to organize and prioritize work. In PULSE and the mobile app, worklists identify animals that need to be seen by dairy workers or animal health experts for specific reasons, such as pregnancy checks or treatment in a hospital pen.

recipe.name: recipe
recipe.description: A combination of ingredients used to make feed for animals

UTC.name: UTC
UTC.description: Coordinated Universal Time, also known as Greenwich Mean Time (GMT)

VISID.name: VISID
VISID.description: Visual ID or ear tag number

Care Package.name: Care Package
Care Package.description: VAS uses the term "Care Package" to describe the updates that the Platform "packages up" and sends to the mobile app. The Care Package is used to keep the mobile app updated with the most recent on-premise data received by the Platform.

Mode.name: Mode
Mode.description: The value that appears most often in a set of data.

dry matter.name: dry matter
dry matter.description: The dry weight of an ingredient with no mositure.

Mixer.name: Mixer
Mixer.description: A piece of farm equipment that mixes all the loaded ingredients for a feed recipe. Mobile mixers are most common, but stationary mixers also exist.

Recipe.name: Recipe
Recipe.description: A combination of ingredients used to make feed for animals

Alter.name: Alter
Alter.description: Command entered to "alter" content or settings in DairyComp. Example: ALTER\7

ABT?.name: ABT?
ABT?.description: ABT? is the code for "Abort?" Often if a dairy sees a pregnant animal showing signs of heat they may breed her. When this breeding is entered in DairyComp the animal remains pregnant, but this flag is set so that the veterinarian can determine if she is still pregnant to the original breeding or if she has aborted and should be updated to another "bred status."

Vet Code 6 = ABT?.name: Vet Code 6 = ABT?
Vet Code 6 = ABT?.description: Vet Code 6 = ABT? Often if a dairy sees a pregnant animal showing signs of heat they may breed her. When this breeding is entered in DairyComp the animal remains pregnant, but this flag is set so that the veterinarian can determine if she is still pregnant to the original breeding or if she has aborted and is now just another "bred status" cow.

Protocol.name: Protocol
Protocol.description: A dairy protocols include a series of treatments or tasks that follow a prescribed order and schedule. Protocols may be defined for reproduction purposes, to treat an illness, or for routine vaccinations.

freemartin.name: freemartin
freemartin.description: A female that is born as a twin or triplet with a bull and may be sterile as a result of exposure to hormones produced by the male in utero.

CAR.name: CAR
CAR.description: Condition Affecting Record. CAR codes are used by DHIA to determine the status of a cow’s record. This includes reason for disposal as well as reasons for the record not to be used for sire proofs. The DairyComp CAR table includes the 9 standard DHIA codes and allows users to add 7 additional codes to the table.

MTAG.name: MTAG
MTAG.description: Metal tag, or USDA tag number for animals in the United States.

tuberculosis.name: tuberculosis
tuberculosis.description: Bovine tuberculosis (TB) is an infectious disease that is caused by the bacteria Mycobacterium bovis (M. bovis).

mastitis.name: mastitis
mastitis.description: Mastitis is the persistent, inflammatory reaction of the udder tissue due to physical trauma or infection. Symptoms of mastitis include udder swelling, heat, hardness, redness, or pain. The milk may have a watery appearance, flakes, clots, or pus.

pneumonia.name: pneumonia
pneumonia.description: Pneumonia is lung inflammation caused by a bacterial or viral infection. Early symptoms may include fever, depression, reduced appetite, nasal and eye discharge, salivation, shallow breathing, and soft coughing.

metritis.name: metritis
metritis.description: Metritis is inflammation of the wall of the uterus, and usually occurs within days of calving. Symptoms of metritis are an enlarged uterus and a foul-smelling uterine discharge.

retained placenta.name: retained placenta
retained placenta.description: Retained placenta is the failure to expel fetal membranes within 24 hours after parturition. The primary symptom of a retained placental is degenerating, discolored, and ultimately fetid membranes hanging from the vulva.

milk fever.name: milk fever
milk fever.description: Milk fever is a disease characterized by reduced blood calcium levels. It occurs in early lactation, when demand for calcium for colostrum and milk production exceeds the cow's ability to mobilize calcium. "Fever" is a misnomer, as body temperature during the disease is generally not elevated. Milk fever symptoms include tremor in the muscles of the head and limbs until falling to a "sitting" position. Once down, cows may have a dry muzzle, staring eyes, cold legs and ears, constipation, and drowsiness.

displaced abomasum.name: displaced abomasum
displaced abomasum.description: Displaced abomasum in cattle occurs when the abomasum, also known as the true stomach, which typically resides on the floor of the abdomen, fills with gas and rises to the top of the abdomen, where it is said to be "displaced". Symptoms may include reduced appetite, lower milk yield, reduced rumination, and a distended abdomen.

pink eye.name: pink eye
pink eye.description: Infectious bovine keratoconjunctivitis (IBK), more commonly known as pink eye, is a highly contagious bacterial infection of the eye.

blood serum.name: blood serum
blood serum.description: Blood serum testing in dairy cattle is used to diagnose disease or for pregnancy testing.

IVF.name: IVF
IVF.description: In-Vitro Fertilization. In relation to embryo transfers, IVF occurs when oocytes (eggs) are collected via OPU (ovum pick up), and then in a lab, semen is added to create embryos.

NAAB.name: NAAB
NAAB.description: National Association of Animal Breeders. www.naab-css.org

TOTP.name: TOTP
TOTP.description: Total protein/solids not fat this lactation

Zone.name: Zone
Zone.description: The term "Zone" represents different dairy locations. Platform Zones may be different physical dairy locations (for example, multiple locations across the state or country) or different user-defined locations within a single dairy site (for example, Pens 1-100 are Zone 1, Pens 101-200 are Zone 2, etc.).

Mastitis.name: Mastitis
Mastitis.description: Mastitis is the persistent, inflammatory reaction of the udder tissue due to physical trauma or infection. Symptoms of mastitis include udder swelling, heat, hardness, redness, or pain. The milk may have a watery appearance, flakes, clots, or pus.

Pneumonia.name: Pneumonia
Pneumonia.description: Pneumonia is lung inflammation caused by a bacterial or viral infection. Early symptoms may include fever, depression, reduced appetite, nasal and eye discharge, salivation, shallow breathing, and soft coughing.

Lame.name: Lame
Lame.description: Lameness includes any abnormality which causes an animal to change the way it walks, and can be caused by a range of foot and leg conditions, including hoof ailments, hock damage, bruising, sores, or cuts.

Scours.name: Scours
Scours.description: Diarrhea in young animals. Scours causes dehydration in calves and is the leading cause of death in calves under one month of age.

Retained Placenta.name: Retained Placenta
Retained Placenta.description: Retained placenta is the failure to expel fetal membranes within 24 hours after parturition. The primary symptom of a retained placental is degenerating, discolored, and ultimately fetid membranes hanging from the vulva.

Ketosis.name: Ketosis
Ketosis.description: Ketosis is a common ailment that usually occurs in early lactation, when the energy demands of milk production are very high and the cow metabolizes fat stores for energy. Symptoms of ketosis may include reduced milk yield, weight loss, reduced appetite, acetone smell of breath and/or milk, and fever.

Milk Fever.name: Milk Fever
Milk Fever.description: Milk fever is a disease characterized by reduced blood calcium levels. It occurs in early lactation, when demand for calcium for colostrum and milk production exceeds the cow's ability to mobilize calcium. "Fever" is a misnomer, as body temperature during the disease is generally not elevated. Milk fever symptoms include tremor in the muscles of the head and limbs until falling to a "sitting" position. Once down, cows may have a dry muzzle, staring eyes, cold legs and ears, constipation, and drowsiness.

Displaced Abomasum.name: Displaced Abomasum
Displaced Abomasum.description: Displaced abomasum in cattle occurs when the abomasum, also known as the true stomach, which typically resides on the floor of the abdomen, fills with gas and rises to the top of the abdomen, where it is said to be "displaced". Symptoms may include reduced appetite, lower milk yield, reduced rumination, and a distended abdomen.

SSF.name: SSF
SSF.description: Standard Send File. This is a text file with animal event and production information used to provide data to a dairy records processing center (DRPC).

DNL.name: DNL
DNL.description: Standard file created by a dairy records processing center (DRPC) that includes the latest status information for each animal in the herd.

standard deviation.name: standard deviation
standard deviation.description: A measure that is used to quantify the amount of variation or dispersion of a set of data values.

median.name: median
median.description: The value separating the higher half from the lower half of a data set.

ALTER.name: ALTER
ALTER.description: Command entered to "alter" content or settings in DairyComp. Example: ALTER\7

RC=4.name: RC=4
RC=4.description: Reproductive code = 4 (BRED). Used to identify animals that have been bred but not do not qualify for open or pregnant status.

RC=2.name: RC=2
RC=2.description: Reproductive code = 2 (FRESH). Used to identify animals that have recently given birth ("fresh").

XID.name: XID
XID.description: XID is short for "Change ID", as in changing an animal's ID number.

task list.name: task list
task list.description: A task list in PULSE and the mobile app is a special type of worklist that is automatically generated for MyDC dairies to capture today's tasks for your herd. The Open Tasks list shows tasks from multiple sources, like your Dairy Protocols and integrations with heat and health partners, and helps you complete all required animal activities for the current day. Task lists include all OvSynch protocols, wellness checks, and any other scheduled tasks.

RC=3.name: RC=3
RC=3.description: Reproductive code = 3 (OK/OPEN). Used to identify animals that are eligible to breed. These could be fresh animals that were checked and declared ready to breed or a bred animal that was declared open.

embryo flush.name: embryo flush
embryo flush.description: Embryos are harvested by being flushed out of a superovulated cow that has already been bred.

LAME.name: LAME
LAME.description: Lameness includes any abnormality which causes an animal to change the way it walks, and can be caused by a range of foot and leg conditions, including hoof ailments, hock damage, bruising, sores, or cuts.

cystic.name: cystic
cystic.description: Cystic ovaries are a reproductive disorder caused by an anovulatory condition in dairy cattle. Ovarian cysts are characterized by large (1 in./2.5 cm) follicles in the animal's ovaries that persist for more than 10 days. Cystic ovaries may lead to infertility or other reproductive issues.

Metritis.name: Metritis
Metritis.description: Metritis is inflammation of the wall of the uterus, and usually occurs within days of calving. Symptoms of metritis are an enlarged uterus and a foul-smelling uterine discharge.

Retained placenta.name: Retained placenta
Retained placenta.description: Retained placenta is the failure to expel fetal membranes within 24 hours after parturition. The primary symptom of a retained placental is degenerating, discolored, and ultimately fetid membranes hanging from the vulva.

Milk fever.name: Milk fever
Milk fever.description: Milk fever is a disease characterized by reduced blood calcium levels. It occurs in early lactation, when demand for calcium for colostrum and milk production exceeds the cow's ability to mobilize calcium. "Fever" is a misnomer, as body temperature during the disease is generally not elevated. Milk fever symptoms include tremor in the muscles of the head and limbs until falling to a "sitting" position. Once down, cows may have a dry muzzle, staring eyes, cold legs and ears, constipation, and drowsiness.

Dry Matter.name: Dry Matter
Dry Matter.description: The dry weight of an ingredient with no mositure.

Health Index.name: Health Index
Health Index.description: Score based on animal age and cost of events to date. The higher the index value, the more likely the animal should be sold.

Health Index Threshold.name: Health Index Threshold
Health Index Threshold.description: Sets the lower limit for index values to include for Health Index Analysis. Animals with an index above this value are included.

VWP.name: VWP
VWP.description: Voluntary wait period, or the number of days a dairy chooses not to breed a cow after calving.

Standard Deviation.name: Standard Deviation
Standard Deviation.description: A measure that is used to quantify the amount of variation or dispersion of a set of data values.

Health index.name: Health index
Health index.description: Score based on animal age and cost of events to date. The higher the index value, the more likely the animal should be sold.

acidosis.name: acidosis
acidosis.description: Sub-acute ruminal acidosis (SARA) is a digestive disorder caused by low rumen pH levels (below 5.6) and diets high in starch and low in fiber. Dairy cows experiencing SARA often do not exhibit any obvious symptoms, though some may have mild diarrhea, reduced appetite, foamy feces, and reduced rumination (cud-chewing).

variance.name: variance
variance.description: A measure of how far each value in the data set is from the mean.

Displaced abomasum.name: Displaced abomasum
Displaced abomasum.description: Displaced abomasum in cattle occurs when the abomasum, also known as the true stomach, which typically resides on the floor of the abdomen, fills with gas and rises to the top of the abdomen, where it is said to be "displaced". Symptoms may include reduced appetite, lower milk yield, reduced rumination, and a distended abdomen.

CDCB.name: CDCB
CDCB.description: Council of Dairy Cattle Breeding is a non-profit organization that calculates and manages genetic evaluations and genomic predictions for dairy cattle.

wean.name: wean
wean.description: A wean event indicates that a calf no longer receives milk for feedings.

replacer.name: replacer
replacer.description: Colostrum replacer is a natural or synthetic product that provides nutrients and antibodies to calves when maternal colostrum is unavailable or of poor quality. It's often used when calves don't nurse from their mother within a few hours of birth.

lame.name: lame
lame.description: Lameness includes any abnormality which causes an animal to change the way it walks, and can be caused by a range of foot and leg conditions, including hoof ailments, hock damage, bruising, sores, or cuts.

trim.name: trim
trim.description: Dairy cattle typically have their hooves inspected and, if necessary, trimmed every 4–6 months. A strategic hoof-trimming program might evaluate hooves before first calving, between 60 and 150 days in milk, and before dry-off.

ketosis.name: ketosis
ketosis.description: Ketosis is a common ailment that usually occurs in early lactation, when the energy demands of milk production are very high and the cow metabolizes fat stores for energy. Symptoms of ketosis may include reduced milk yield, weight loss, reduced appetite, acetone smell of breath and/or milk, and fever.

Standard deviation.name: Standard deviation
Standard deviation.description: A measure that is used to quantify the amount of variation or dispersion of a set of data values.

STAT.name: STAT
STAT.description: DHIA (Dairy Herd Improvement Association) status code

DHIA.name: DHIA
DHIA.description: The Dairy Herd Improvement Association (DHIA) is a national association that helps dairy producers create and manage records and data about their cows for use in making management decisions. See http://www.dhia.org/ for more information.

INT.name: INT
INT.description: The abbreviation INT has several meanings. Depending on the context within this online help, it means either Interval or Internal.

PTOTP.name: PTOTP
PTOTP.description: Previous lactation total protein/solids not fat

DCCP.name: DCCP
DCCP.description: Days carried calf at pregnancy check

Billing Management.name: Billing Management
Billing Management.description: Allows you to manage the dairy's billing account for the VAS PULSE Platform.

Dashboard Management.name: Dashboard Management
Dashboard Management.description: Allows you to create new Dairy Dashboards and customize the widgets displayed on existing ones.

Dashboard View.name: Dashboard View
Dashboard View.description: Allows you to view the Dairy Dashboard.

File Management.name: File Management
File Management.description: Allows you to upload, download, and manage shared files, including the ability to create, edit, delete, and view file history details, in any domain.

General.name: General
General.description: Allows you to download and view history details for general shared files not associated with Herd, Feed, and Parlor data.

Integration Management.name: Integration Management
Integration Management.description: Allows you to manage Platform integration with other applications that send data to or extract data from Platform (for example, DairyComp for DC Unlimited subscribers, FeedComp, Parlors, and other allowed 3rd‑party software integrations).

Profile Management.name: Profile Management
Profile Management.description: Allows you to edit the dairy profile settings under Dairy Configuration > Dairy Information (dairy description, discoverability, address, etc.).

User Management.name: User Management
User Management.description: Allows you to invite new users, remove existing users, and manage role and permission settings for other users.

Zone Management.name: Zone Management
Zone Management.description: Allows you to create and manage dairy Zones. The term "Zone" represents different dairy locations. Platform Zones may be different physical dairy locations (for example, multiple locations across the state or country) or different user-defined locations within a single dairy site (for example, Pens 1-100 are Zone 1, Pens 101-200 are Zone 2, etc.).

description: Allows you to edit configuration settings, including items, events, commands, breeding codes, reproduction codes, etc.

Adjust DM%.description: Allows you to adjust dry matter % in FeedComp.

Access.description: Allows you to manage Inventory in FeedComp.

Mobile App Access.name: Mobile App Access
Mobile App Access.description: Allows you to download and use FeedComp.

Feeding Amounts.description: Allows you to edit Pen Feeding Amounts in FeedComp.

Adjust Amounts.description: Allows you to edit Ingredient Amounts in FeedComp.

Set Alternate.name: Set Alternate
Set Alternate.description: Allows you to set Alternate Recipes in FeedComp.

Task list.name: Task list
Task list.description: A task list in PULSE and the mobile app is a special type of worklist that is automatically generated for MyDC dairies to capture today's tasks for your herd. The Open Tasks list shows tasks from multiple sources, like your Dairy Protocols and integrations with heat and health partners, and helps you complete all required animal activities for the current day. Task lists include all OvSynch protocols, wellness checks, and any other scheduled tasks.

Events.21CULTURE.content: CULTURE Culture events are typically used to identify the source of mastitis and high SCC testday results.

masterVAS-Terminology.c-olostrum.content: colostrum Colostrum, the first milk produced by a cow after birth, is high in nutrients and antibodies that provide calves initial protection against disease.

Insights.HealthIndexAnalysis.content: Health Index Score based on animal age and cost of events to date. The higher the index value, the more likely the animal should be sold. Analysis

RolesPermissions.File_Management.name: RolesPermissions.File_Management
RolesPermissions.File_Management.content: Allows you to upload, download, and manage shared files, including the ability to create, edit, delete, and view file history details, in any domain.

RolesPermissions.General_File_Download.name: RolesPermissions.General_File_Download
RolesPermissions.General_File_Download.content: Allows you to download and view history details for general shared files not associated with Herd, Feed, and Parlor data.

RolesPermissions.User_Management.name: RolesPermissions.User_Management
RolesPermissions.User_Management.content: Allows you to invite new users, remove existing users, and manage role and permission settings for other users.

RolesPermissions.Zone_Management.name: RolesPermissions.Zone_Management
RolesPermissions.Zone_Management.content: Allows you to create and manage dairy Zones.

RolesPermissions.ZoneDefinition.name: RolesPermissions.ZoneDefinition
RolesPermissions.ZoneDefinition.content: The term "Zone" represents different dairy locations. Platform Zones may be different physical dairy locations (for example, multiple locations across the state or country) or different user-defined locations within a single dairy site (for example, Pens 1-100 are Zone 1, Pens 101-200 are Zone 2, etc.).

masterVAS-Terminology.P-rotocolMgmt-.name: masterVAS-Terminology.P-rotocolMgmt-
masterVAS-Terminology.P-rotocolMgmt-.content: Protocol A dairy protocols include a series of treatments or tasks that follow a prescribed order and schedule. Protocols may be defined for reproduction purposes, to treat an illness, or for routine vaccinations. Management

RolesPermissions.Herd_Protocol_Managemen.name: RolesPermissions.Herd_Protocol_Management
RolesPermissions.Herd_Protocol_Managemen.content: Allows you to create and edit treatment protocols and prescriptions for animals with illnesses.

RolesPermissions.Herd_Read.name: RolesPermissions.Herd_Read
RolesPermissions.Herd_Read.content: Allows you to view zones, animals, herd integration status, and reports.

RolesPermissions.Herd_Write.name: RolesPermissions.Herd_Write
RolesPermissions.Herd_Write.content: Allows you to enter and update animal details, status, items, events, and testdays. May require Zone permissions as well.

Widgets.DryMatterIntake.name: Widgets.DryMatterIntake
Widgets.DryMatterIntake.content: Dry Matter Intake by Pen Type

RC=0.name: RC=0
RC=0.description: Reproductive Code = 0. Used for young calves and heifers that have not been bred.

RC=1.name: RC=1
RC=1.description: Reproductive code = 1 (NO BRED). Used to identify Do Not Breed (DNB) animals.

RC=5.name: RC=5
RC=5.description: Reproductive code = 5 (PREG). Used to identify animals that have been declared pregnant.

RC=6.name: RC=6
RC=6.description: Reproductive code = 6 (DRY). Used to identify pregnant animals that have stopped milking.

health index.name: health index
health index.description: Score based on animal age and cost of events to date. The higher the index value, the more likely the animal should be sold.
